  "findings": [
    "Flashing is not lapped or sealed at joints, creating a pathway for water penetration into the building envelope."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on masonry veneer installation in the affected areas.",
    "Expose all unsealed flashing joints.",
    "Lap flashing a minimum of 6 inches and seal with compatible adhesive/mastic per manufacturer specifications and project submittals."
  ],
  "rationale": "Flashing must be continuous and properly lapped/sealed to direct water to the exterior via weep holes. Unsealed flashing joints will allow water to bypass the flashing system, leading to water intrusion, mold, and structural damage behind the masonry veneer.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402",
    "BIA Technical Notes 7",
    "IBC Chapter 14"
  ]
}
